message:
fix(abi,membrane): replace dlvsym-based memcpy/memmove/memset with std intrinsics and add fork safety

Replace raw_memcpy_bytes, raw_memmove_bytes, and raw_memset_bytes
implementations that used dlvsym to resolve glibc symbols at runtime
with std::ptr::copy_nonoverlapping, std::ptr::copy, and
std::ptr::write_bytes respectively. The dlvsym approach triggered a
PageOracle RwLock deadlock during membrane initialization because
the interposed dlsym/strlen paths re-entered the membrane before it
was ready. The std intrinsics compile to inline rep movsb/stosb on
x86_64, avoiding any dynamic symbol resolution entirely.

Add atfork_prepare() to AllocationArena and ValidationPipeline that
acquires all shard locks before a fork, ensuring the arena is in a
consistent state when the child process inherits memory. Returns an
ArenaAtforkGuard that releases locks on drop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
